Oar *Kra: 2411 rl -?"4r-ird 41911 IN'it 'ow *r rti*T akr * e(4)th TM" *1- 4.14-N1 4%.;.-44 sk4 f 1- 4 5 0 I 1/ i 7/9 2 87-01-anf-et-41--Ift-Tn:F. aiTTPT 71)-4-4, 41 act Rtir4 2934 GI/95 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- THE GAZE n1-, OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART 11—SEC, 3(iin _ MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 8th December, 1995 S.O. 963(E).--In exercise of the powers conferred by Sub-section (1) of Section 154 of the Indo-Tibetan Border Police Force Act, 1992 (35 of 1992), the Central Government hereby directs (1) that the powers conferred and duties imposed on a police officer by Section 41(1)-, 46, 47, 48, 49, 51(1), 52, 74, 102, 149, 150, 151 and 152 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 (2 of 1974), or in the State of Jammu and Kashmir • Code of Criminal Procedure, 1889, shall be the powers and duties respectively of a member of the Indo-Tibetan Border Police Force of the corresponding or equivalent rank, and (2) that the powers conferred and duties imposed on a police .0ficer by Sections 53, 100, 129 of the Code of Criminal 'Procedure, 1973 (2 of 1974), or in the State of Jammu and Kashmir 400the corresponding Sections of the Jammu and Kashmir Code of Criminal Procedure, 1889 shall be the powers and duties, respectively of every member of and above the rank of Sub-Inspector of the Indo-Tibetan Border Police Force. [File No. I. 45011/17/92(L)-ITBPF] ANURAG GOEL, Jt. Secy. 2 Printed by the Manager.
